2604.25796.title: StratFormer: Adaptive Opponent Modeling and Exploitation in Imperfect-Information Games
2604.25796.paper_summary: We present StratFormer, a transformer-based meta-agent that learns to simultaneously model and exploit opponents in imperfect-information games through a two-phase curriculum. The first phase trains an opponent modeling head to identify behavioral patterns from action histories while the agent plays a game-theoretic optimal (GTO) policy. The second phase progressively shifts the policy toward best-response (BR) exploitation, guided by a per-opponent regularization schedule tied to exploitability. Our architecture introduces dual-turn tokens -- feature vectors constructed at both agent and opponent decision points -- coupled with bucket-rate features that encode opponent tendencies across five strategic contexts. On Leduc Hold'em, a small poker variant with six cards and two betting rounds, we test against six opponent archetypes at two strength levels each, with exploitability ranging from 0.15 to 1.26 Big Blinds (BB) per hand. StratFormer achieves an average exploitation gain of +0.106 BB per hand over GTO, with peak gains of +0.821 against highly exploitable opponents, while maintaining near-equilibrium safety.
2604.25796.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了StratFormer，一個基於Transformer的元代理，通過兩階段課程學習同時建模和利用在不完全信息遊戲中的對手。第一階段訓練一個對手建模頭，從行動歷史中識別行為模式，同時代理執行博弈論最優（GTO）策略。第二階段逐步將策略轉向最佳回應（BR）利用，並根據與可利用性相關的每個對手正則化計劃進行指導。我們的架構引入了雙回合令牌——在代理和對手決策點構建的特徵向量——結合了編碼對手在五個戰略背景下傾向的桶率特徵。在Leduc Hold'em這個小型撲克變體中，該遊戲有六張牌和兩輪下注，我們測試了六種對手原型，每種對手有兩個強度級別，其可利用性範圍從每手0.15到1.26大盲注（BB）。StratFormer在GTO之上實現了每手平均+0.106 BB的利用增益，對於高度可利用的對手，峰值增益達到+0.821，同時保持接近均衡的安全性。
2604.25796.author: Andy Caen et.al.
2604.25796.authors: Andy Caen, Mark H. M. Winands, Dennis J. N. J. Soemers
